subject: otters
one_liner: Raft matches sea otters with hand-holding partners vetted for grip strength, rock taste, and nap drift, so nobody wakes up alone in open water.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan matchmaking-platform corporate. Every metric is stated with total confidence and zero sourcing. Trust and safety theater (rock verification, drift insurance) is treated as the company's proudest achievement. Never acknowledges that the users are otters. Never winks.

hero.sub: Raft pairs you with otters whose grip strength, sleep schedule, and shellfish preferences are statistically unlikely to end in drift. 94% of matched pairs are still within one kelp strand after seven naps.

features.title: Grip Compatibility Index
features.blurb: Our matching algorithm scores paw-to-paw retention across 14 grip dimensions, including wet torque, mid-nap slack, and recovery time after a wave event. Pairs scoring below 6.0 are never introduced. We consider this a kindness.

features.title: Rock Verification
features.blurb: Every member's pouch rock is photographed, weighed, and authenticated by our Trust team before their profile goes live. Posing with a borrowed rock is a lifetime ban, no appeals. We take rock fraud more seriously than any platform in this category.

features.title: DriftGuard
features.blurb: If a matched pair exceeds 40 meters of overnight drift, both parties receive a wake-up nudge and one prepaid strand of anchoring kelp, delivered to the nearer otter. Included on all annual plans. Claims are processed while you nap.

pricing_tiers.name: Floater
pricing_tiers.tagline: For otters who insist they're fine out there alone.

pricing_tiers.name: Raft+
pricing_tiers.tagline: Our most popular plan among the recently drifted.

pricing_tiers.name: Apex Mustelid
pricing_tiers.tagline: For otters who are done floating solo. Permanently.
perks: Profile boosted to every raft within 2 nautical miles
Your rock featured on our homepage

testimonials.quote: I matched with Gary on a Tuesday. By Thursday we had drifted only 11 meters overnight. The numbers don't lie.
testimonials.name: Pebbles
testimonials.title: Senior Rock Curator, Monterey Bay Raft #7

testimonials.quote: After my last partner let go mid-nap, I didn't think I could trust again. The Grip Compatibility Index gave me a framework for that trust.
testimonials.name: Gary
testimonials.title: Recently Re-Paired, Elkhorn Slough

testimonials.quote: The rock verification process was invasive and took nine days. Then they featured my rock on the homepage, and honestly, it was all worth it.
testimonials.name: Denise
testimonials.title: Apex Mustelid Member Since March, Morro Bay

demo_screen_concept: A swipeable card stack of otter profiles. Each card shows a polaroid-framed avatar, name, age, home raft, distance in nautical miles, a Grip Compatibility score out of 10, a verified-rock badge with the rock's name and weight, and three interest chips. Two actions: 'let drift' (pass) and 'hold hands' (like), via buttons or drag-to-swipe. Holding hands with a profile scoring 8.0+ triggers an 'It's a raft!' match modal confirming one prepaid kelp strand has been reserved. A top status bar shows DriftGuard armed status and remaining daily paw-holds, which actually decrement.

design_direction.style: Sun-faded 1970s tide-pool postcard / retro film. A sun-bleached apricot paper ground under a fine film-grain overlay, chunky rounded retro shapes, tilted polaroid-framed profile cards, wavy section dividers, and souvenir-sticker feature badges. Deep kelp teal carries structure and full-bleed section bands; one faded sunset coral is the only action color. Flat print textures throughout: no 3D, no blur, no neon, no gradients beyond a gentle sun-fade wash. Reads like a beach-motel postcard rack that has been acquired by a venture-backed matchmaking platform.
design_direction.archetype: Dating-app landing rendered as a souvenir postcard rack: polaroid-stack hero, three sticker-plate features, three postcard testimonials with stamp corners, a motel-rate pricing rack, and a demo handoff to the swipe deck.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Slow bobbing float on the hero polaroids, tilt-straighten on card hover, 200ms ease-out color shifts on CTAs, gentle scroll fade-ins. All motion disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Split hero on the apricot ground: left column carries the flush-left Abril Fatface headline, the drift statistic subline, and the coral CTA; right column is a fanned stack of three tilted polaroid profile cards with grip scores and a verified-rock sticker badge. A wavy teal divider hands off into a full-bleed teal features band.
